evidence: Rediscovering Rossellini is always a great way to spend some time in front of the TV.
Any history buff will want to watch these episodes.
The dialogues are absolutely ridiculous, in terms of the words used.
The directing of the actors seems lifeless.
Too often the directorial trend is more of a theater-like delivery, instead of a more life-like.
A lot of action, not very well coordinated, and a very thin intrigue.
the sound is bad in Italian, worse in German and worst in English.
the quality of the two transfers is dismal.
I think criterion needs to redo the second disc.
Don't be fooled by the Criterion label.
the cinematography is awesome, excellent acting and photography.
the audio, at least of my copy, has a very annoying click.
I would not recommend it in this version.

additional_patterns.name: disappointment_with_dubbing
evidence: I think it’s a bad idea to have it dubbed in English instead of having subtitles.
it is terrible that, in 2005, anybody would dare to release such a poor quality DVD.

summary: The user shows a strong preference for historical and documentary genres, often appreciating the content but expressing significant dissatisfaction with production quality. They are sensitive to audio and visual elements, indicating a high quality sensitivity, while their emotional engagement appears to be low, focusing more on technical aspects than on narrative depth.
